_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in recognizing significant changes in a target’s communication patterns.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences, including paper writing, conference presentation, and internal briefing formats.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-A-003 (RC, cosine: 0.393), LS-S-007 (LS, cosine: 0.389).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.1/10 (raw 0.308). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. DCWF-only terms [changes, communication, patterns, recognizing, s, significant] vs WIDAI-only terms [audiences, briefing, communicating, conference, findings, formats] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.879 (P=0.899, R=0.860). P exceeds R by 0.039 — the DCWF element's 10 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.9%). "Skill in recognizing significant changes in a target’s communication patterns." and "Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical aud" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.445 > STS 0.308 (gap +0.137). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.4%. Shared tokens: [skill]. DCWF-only: [changes, communication, patterns, recognizing, s, significant]. WIDAI-only: [audiences, briefing, communicating, conference, findings, formats].
